source_name: Selected Australian defence alliance and strategic frameworks
notes: Manual compilation from official public treaty, Defence, DFAT, ONI and Australian Submarine Agency pages. Formal alliances, intelligence partnerships and diplomatic forums are kept separate.
source_rights: Commonwealth copyright; upstream source rights remain with each publisher
citation: DFAT Australian Treaties Database; DFAT Quad and AUSMIN pages; Office of National Intelligence Five Eyes partners page; Australian Submarine Agency AUKUS pages.

frameworks.formal_status: In force for Australia according to the Australian Treaties Database
frameworks.purpose_summary: Security treaty between Australia, New Zealand and the United States, with Australia-US commitment reaffirmed after the US suspension of obligations to New Zealand in 1986.
frameworks.notes: Treaty status and dates are treaty-record facts. This dashboard does not model operational decisions under the treaty.

frameworks.purpose_summary: Supports Australia's pathway to conventionally armed nuclear-powered submarines and deeper industrial and advanced capability cooperation.
frameworks.notes: AUKUS is not represented here as a mutual defence treaty. Nuclear-propulsion material is handled under separate legal and safeguards arrangements.

frameworks.purpose_summary: ONI describes Five Eyes as Australia's longest-standing intelligence collaboration, supporting intelligence coverage across missions including counter-terrorism, counter-proliferation, cyber and regional challenges.
frameworks.notes: The public ONI page supports the high-level description, but detailed intelligence-sharing mechanics are not public and are not modelled.

frameworks.formal_status: Diplomatic partnership, not a formal alliance
frameworks.purpose_summary: DFAT describes the Quad as a diplomatic partnership focused on a peaceful, stable and prosperous Indo-Pacific, including maritime and transnational security, economic security, critical technologies, cyber security and emergency response.
frameworks.notes: The dashboard labels Quad as a diplomatic partnership, not a defence treaty.

frameworks.purpose_summary: DFAT's 2025 AUSMIN fact sheet records Australia-US commitments across Indo-Pacific security, AUKUS, force posture cooperation, technology and regional resilience.
frameworks.notes: AUSMIN is a consultation mechanism supporting the alliance; it is not itself a separate mutual defence treaty.

frameworks.framework_name: Papua New Guinea-Australia Mutual Defence Treaty
frameworks.formal_status: Treaty text published by DFAT; entry into force depends on treaty notifications
frameworks.purpose_summary: DFAT publishes the treaty text as a bilateral mutual defence treaty between Australia and Papua New Guinea, also known as the Pukpuk Treaty.
frameworks.notes: The page text is public, but the dashboard does not model implementation status beyond the published source notes.

fields.boundary: Treaties, intelligence partnerships, trilateral technology partnerships, diplomatic forums and ministerial consultations are different framework types and are not collapsed into one alliance score.
